visual_summary: BlurFade is a component for smoothly fading in and out of view, blurring the content as it disappears. It's ideal for hiding content until the user scrolls into view. The component can be customized with different blur levels and fade directions.
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready other component with the following characteristics:

Name: Blur Fade
Description: BlurFade is a component for smoothly fading in and out of view, blurring the content as it disappears. It's ideal for hiding content until the user scrolls into view. The component can be customized with different blur levels and fade directions.
Style: minimal, glassmorphism
Interactions: animated
Tags: animation, fading, blur, visibility, scrolling

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
Reference implementation (adapt don't copy):
"use client"

import { useRef } from "react"
import {
  AnimatePresence,
  motion,
  useInView,
  type MotionProps,
  type UseInViewOptions,
  type Variants,
} from "motion/react"

type MarginType = UseInViewOptions["margin"]

interface BlurFadeProps extends MotionProps {
  children: React.ReactNode
  className?: string
  variant?: {
    hidden: { y: number }
    visible: { y: number }
  }
  duration?: number
  delay?: number
  offset?: number
  direction?: "up" | "down" | "left" | "right"
  inV
